mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, validating corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which reinforces short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods following INDPRO increases, or the signal predictive power vanishes during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated policy rates and persistent inflation creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and increases discount rates, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 60-day returns in > 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the 2.0% threshold confirms robust economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the threshold, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand, which boosts consumer spending expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production contracts while retail sales expands signals an unsustainable inventory drawdown and supply bottleneck, triggering corporate margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the mechanism fails to predict returns during periods of stable supply chains.
